Tuberculosis is all over the Malaysian news again. But here’s the truth most people don’t realise. TB never left. Malaysia records ~26k TB cases every year. Sabah carries one of the highest burdens and being here for the last 8 years, Ive seen tb in its many forms, from the common tb in lungs to tb in marrow, eye, even genitals. This isn’t a sudden outbreak, it’s a persistent, slow burn epidemic that has been with us for decades. The screening is way more effective now.

Quick question, sekarang kes TB meningkat. Patient datang clinic suspect anak TB. Walaupun takde symptoms of TB tapi dia nak check jugak sebab anak batuk. At GP setting kita boleh proceed dgn apa ya. Mintak tolong. 1. Kita refer KK buat mantoux text ke? 2. Buat xray ke ? 3. Send sputum AFB x3 ke ?

if you hv recent contact with TB n no symptom, you can do Mantoux test or TB quantiferon test (blood) and a chest X ray to see if you hv latent TB infection. But if you hv symptoms of prolonged cough, a chest X ray and sputum smear for acid fast bacilli (AFB) x3 +/- sputum for TB PCR is needed to see if it is active TB; active TB is either smear positive TB if AFB is positive (very highly super contagious) or smear negative TB (contagious too). All test is available @eden.family.clinic_puchong

1/4 “Conjunctivitis” sounds like such a fancy medical term. But it’s really just a word invented to describe a range of symptoms. It’s an inflammation of the conjunctiva, the thin membrane that lines the eyelid and covers the white part of the eyeball. This condition can cause redness, swelling, and discomfort in the eye. There are several types of conjunctivitis: Viral Conjunctivitis: Often caused by viruses like the common cold. It is highly contagious and usually resolves on its own.

Here are some common types of headaches:
1. Tension headache: dull, aching pain on both sides of the head, often stress-related.
2. Migraine: throbbing pain, often one-sided, with nausea, sensitivity to light/sound.
3. Cluster headache: severe, piercing pain on one side of the head, often around the eye.
4. Sinus headache: pain in cheeks, forehead, due to sinus inflammation.
5. Rebound headache: caused by overusing pain med

Dear HO, here are some tips to clerk pt with chest pain.
"OPQRST"
1. 0 - Onset (Sudden or gradual - ask for duration, U/L medical hx)
2. P - Provocation/Palliation (What makes it worse/better - ask about eating habits- think abt gastritis)
3. Q - Quality (Sharp, dull, squeezing, burning, reflux)
4. R - Radiation (To arm, jaw, back- can be pancreatitis/ cholelithiasis)
5. S - Severity (Scale of 1-10- can show the pain scale)
6. T - Timing (Intermittent, continuous, related to exertion?)

1/4 “Conjunctivitis” sounds like such a fancy medical term. But it’s really just a word invented to describe a range of symptoms.
It’s an inflammation of the conjunctiva, the thin membrane that lines the eyelid and covers the white part of the eyeball. This condition can cause redness, swelling, and discomfort in the eye.
There are several types of conjunctivitis:
Viral Conjunctivitis: Often caused by viruses like the common cold. It is highly contagious and usually resolves on its own.
